title: Work Out Your Own Salvation
summary: “Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling. For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.”(Philippians 2:12-13). James Strong defines “work out” in the following manner: κατεργάζομαι katergázomai, kat-er-gad'-zom-ahee; from G2596 and G2038; to work fully, i.e. accomplish; by implication, to finish, fashion:—cause, to (deed), perform, work (out). One can liken salvation to a package that contains instructions to navigate and negotiate the challenges in this world with the aim of making it to heaven. There is only one salvation accomplished by the Lord Jesus Christ through his divine identity and atonement for our sins at Golgotha. “And being made perfect, he became the author of eternal salvation unto all them that obey him” (Hebrews 5:9). The Lord Jesus accomplished his work of collective salvation when he died on the Cross, gave up the ghost, and said, “It is finished” (John 19:30). However, each one of us will find that the package of salvation leads us on unique paths that sometimes overlap, but often diverge. Hence, the words of the Apostle Paul, “work out your own salvation with fear and trembling”. In fact, much of the Bible is a compilation of individuals who worked out their own salvation, and, according to Hebrews 11:2 “obtained a good report”. Let us consider some of these individuals and the way they worked out their own salvation and be inspired.
